habitat.text: China, Southern Europe, and cultivated in England in hot-houses.

keynotes.text: A half-hardy herbaceous climbing, half-shrubby plant, with large trusses of pale-blue flowers, which are in bloom continuously through the summer. This variety is also known under the name of Plumbago Capensis , and is greatly used in German gardening.

constituents.text: Plumbagin, a crystallizable acrid principle obtained from the root.

indications.text: It is acrid, and when chewed creates a free flow of saliva, particularly if root is used; said to be of benefit to relieve toothache, and has long been used in France for that purpose, hence its name, dentalaire; also useful for itch - a decoction of the root in olive oil is much used.

generalities.text: P. Zeylanica is said to be a strong diaphoretic.
